questions.question: What is meant by improvement in food resources?
questions.correct_answer: Increasing the production of crops and livestock using scientific methods
questions.explanation: Improvement in food resources involves better farming practices and livestock management to meet the food demand of a growing population.

questions.question: What are cereals mainly used for in our diet?
questions.explanation: Cereals such as rice, wheat and maize are rich sources of carbohydrates.

questions.question: What are pulses an important source of?
questions.explanation: Pulses such as gram, peas and lentils are rich in protein and are important for body growth.

questions.question: What is hybridisation in crop variety improvement?
questions.explanation: Hybridisation helps combine desirable traits like higher yield and disease resistance.

questions.question: What are macronutrients in plants?
questions.correct_answer: Nutrients required by plants in large quantities
questions.explanation: Examples include nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium, magnesium and sulphur.

questions.question: What is irrigation?
questions.correct_answer: The artificial supply of water to crops
questions.explanation: Irrigation ensures crops receive adequate water for growth even when rainfall is insufficient.

questions.question: What is mixed cropping?
questions.correct_answer: Growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same piece of land
questions.explanation: Mixed cropping reduces the risk of crop failure and improves productivity.

questions.question: What are weeds?
questions.correct_answer: Unwanted plants that grow along with crops
questions.explanation: Weeds compete with crops for nutrients, sunlight and water.

questions.question: What is animal husbandry?
questions.explanation: It includes feeding, breeding and disease control of animals like cattle, poultry and fish.

questions.question: What is composite fish culture?
questions.correct_answer: Raising different fish species together in a single pond
questions.explanation: Different species use different food resources in the pond, increasing overall fish production.

notes.title: Crop Variety Improvement
notes.content: Crop variety improvement focuses on developing crop varieties with higher yield, better quality and resistance to diseases and environmental stresses.

notes.title: Manure
notes.content: Manure is organic matter obtained from plant and animal waste that improves soil fertility and structure.

notes.title: Fertilizers
notes.content: Fertilizers are commercially produced chemicals that supply nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium to plants.

notes.title: Animal Husbandry
notes.content: Animal husbandry includes cattle farming, poultry farming, fisheries and bee-keeping to produce food and other useful products.

notes.title: Bee-Keeping
notes.content: Bee-keeping or apiculture is the maintenance of bee colonies to produce honey and wax.

facts.fact: India's food production increased greatly after the Green Revolution.
Macronutrients are required in large quantities while micronutrients are needed in small quantities.
Crop rotation helps maintain soil fertility and reduce pest problems.
Composite fish culture increases fish yield by using different feeding levels in the pond.
